payload.title: Frictionless adoption — one command, zero ceremony
payload.description: Collapse the getting-started story from the README's multi-step env-var + dual-activation dance down to: install one tool, build with one command. Key insight grounding this epic: Trust has NO custom compiler — trust-rustc/trust-rustdoc are thin RUSTC_WRAPPER/RUSTDOC shims that lower source-to-source (syn rewrite) and exec stock rustc (see crates/trust-rustc/src/lib.rs). So all current friction is activation + wiring ceremony, not an essential toolchain dependency, and can be hidden. Today's friction: (1) two ways to activate strict mode (#![strict] for single files vs trust_attrs::strict!{} for cargo crates), (2) manual export RUSTC_WRAPPER + RUSTDOC, (3) a trust-attrs path dependency. Target end state: `cargo install cargo-trust` + `strict = true` in Cargo.toml + `cargo trust build`.
